scope: The SwornAgent TUI operator can discover and open non-HEAD release boards using the S01 catalog snapshot, preserve selected sourceRef identity for async loads, and render explicit durability markers from the shared state-evidence fields.

test_results.output_ref: 2026-07-18 live run: internal/tui and internal/board passed
verdict PASS; all four ACs mapped to integration evidence
verdict PASS; AC-01 through AC-04 satisfied

reachability.evidence: 2026-07-18 live runs from the track worktree passed the targeted TUI/board/CLI test set and the full Go suite. Reachability is exercised by TestRefOnlyReleaseListAndBoardUsesCatalogSnapshot, TestRefOnlyBoardAndCLIStateEvidenceAgree, TestBoardViewRendersCatalogStateDurability, TestReleasesListRendersCatalogUncommittedAggregate, TestReleasesListUsesSharedNonGitCatalogFallback, TestRefAwareReleaseInteractionContract, and TestRefOnlyBoardLoadRejectsStaleDifferentSourceRef.

delivered.item: AC-01 and AC-04 — release discovery and async board load now flow from catalog records and reject stale sourceRef board payloads.
AC-02 — `[uncommitted]` is rendered from state durability and aggregate sourceRef-based labels.
AC-03 and AC-04 error/selection paths avoid filesystem fallback parser and maintain deterministic async transition state.
